business.headline: US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

Waiting for Form 16? Here's when you'll receive it and why it matters for your ITRThe countdown to ITR filing has begun, and Form 16 remains one of the most important documents for salaried taxpayers. Knowing when your employer must issue it and what information it contains can help you prepare for a smoother tax filing experience.

Sensex ends 382 points higher, Nifty nears 23,500 led by IT sector rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 382.50 points, or 0.52%, to close at 74,649.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 100.95 points, or 0.43%, to settle at 23,483.55.

From 5th to 7th: Why India is losing the stock market race against Asian rivalsIndia has slipped from fifth to seventh in global stock market rankings as South Korea and Taiwan moved ahead. The shift shows investors are favouring AI-linked semiconductor hubs over markets with limited listed chipmakers.

8th Pay Commission deadline extended again: What it means for your salary and arrearsAnother extension, more questions. The 8th Pay Commission has pushed back its submission deadline again, keeping employees guessing about pay hikes and arrears.

Will your home loan EMI rise again? What RBI's June MPC meet could mean for borrowersIf you have a home loan, the RBI's June policy meeting could be worth watching closely. Borrowers are eager to know whether their EMIs will remain stable or face another change in the months ahead.

Why is Nvidia hiring when the rest of Silicon Valley is firingNvidia is expanding H-1B hiring even as major technology rivals cut jobs and slow foreign recruitment. The move stands out for Indian professionals facing layoffs, visa pressure and a tighter US market.

Why your society maintenance bill could soon be Rs 3,000 higher every monthFor many apartment residents, the next surprise could arrive not in their power bill or fuel bill, but in their society maintenance statement. Rising wage costs and higher operating expenses are forcing several housing societies to consider significant fee hikes.

India-US trade talks begin today: Why Section 301 could become the biggest issueIndian and US negotiators have begun fresh trade talks in New Delhi with Section 301 emerging as a central issue. India wants assurance that future US tariff action will not erode gains from a bilateral trade deal.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech jump up to 6%. 3 reasons why IT stocks are rallying today?IT stocks extended gains for a third straight session, led by Infosys, TCS, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra. The rally reflects stronger global tech sentiment, AI spending optimism, rate-cut hopes and support from a weaker rupee.

After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India in global stock market rankingsSouth Korea has moved past India in global stock market rankings after a sharp rally in AI-linked chipmakers. The shift underlines how investors are favouring markets tied to the AI supply chain while Indian equities face near-term pressures.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,300; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 302.16 points, or 0.41%, to 73,965.18 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 107.05 points, or 0.46%, to 23,275.55 as of 9:25 am.

Trump cuts tariffs on farm, industrial equipment from 25% to 15%. What it meansUS President Donald Trump has lowered US tariffs on selected farm and industrial equipment from 25% to 15% through December 2027. The move couples cheaper imports with incentives for greater use of American steel and aluminium.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

India may reconsider Scotch whisky tariff cuts if UK imposes steel curbsIndia could revisit tariff concessions granted to Britain on products such as Scotch whisky if London fails to address New Delhi's concerns over proposed steel restrictions, according to an Indian official.

Can small-cap funds really deliver higher long-term returns?While small caps have delivered better returns than large caps over the past two decades, the difference may be smaller than many investors expect, says a recent study.

SIP investing, the RCB way18 years. That's how long RCB supporters waited for glory. If patience eventually paid off in cricket, could the same principle be the secret behind successful SIP investing?

What is Section 301 of the US Trade Act? Why India wants protection from itAs negotiations enter the final stage, one issue attracting attention is India's effort to secure protection from possible US tariffs under Section 301 of the US Trade Act, 1974.

entertainment.headline: Actors' body reacts to non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh in Don 3 row Actors' body CINTAA has raised concerns over how the dispute involving Ranveer Singh and FWICE was handled, saying it was not taken into confidence before the situation escalated publicly. Members also stressed the need for dialogue and a proper dispute-resolution system to handle such conflicts in the film industry.

Shilpa Shinde says her sexual harassment claims against Bhabiji producer were false Actor Shilpa Shinde has made a revelation regarding her past legal dispute linked to Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain!. She said that the sexual harassment allegations she made against producer Sanjay Kohli were false and were part of a larger conflict at the time.

Badshah's Chandigarh nightclub sealed over building violations Singer-rapper Badshah's nightclub SAGO in Chandigarh's Sector 26 has been sealed by the administration over alleged unauthorised constructions and building norm violations.

I shouldn't have laughed: Pranit More apologises over 'peak Gurgaon content' clip Pranit More apologised after a stand-up clip showed him laughing at an audience member's remarks about "recovering" the cost of a date, triggering online backlash.

Dua Lipa, Callum Turner make it official in wedding pics that scream peak romance Dua Lipa and Callum Turner made their wedding official with dreamy photos on social media. The singer shared glimpses from their intimate London ceremony, offering fans the first official look at her special day.

Kangana's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata trailer packs terror and survival in 26/11 story The trailer of Bharat Bhagya Vidhata, starring Kangana Ranaut, offers a glimpse into the courage of the nurses who protected patients during the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.
Success brings enemies: Kangana Ranaut backs Ranveer Singh amid Don 3 ban row Kangana Ranaut backed Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 ban row. She said success often brings enemies and obstacles.

Euphoria 3 finale: Not just Rue, the series failed all its women As Euphoria reached its final stretch, its women increasingly became symbols rather than people. That shift left the series facing questions about whether it examined female experience or merely packaged it as spectacle.

Ram Charan's lookalike fan causes chaos at Peddi event, gets surprise reward later A fan dressed like Ram Charan rushed towards the stage at the Peddi pre-release event in Vijayawada before being stopped by the actor's personal security guard. The lookalike fan was later allowed to meet the actor.

Diljit Dosanjh's Aroma song leaves bitter aftertaste with use of transgender slur Diljit Dosanjh has come under fire for using a transgender slur in his song Aroma. The row gained momentum at the start of Pride Month, with many questioning the message and implications of the lyric.

Comedian Sumukhi Suresh on bid to stay relevant: Pulling my hair every day Comedian Sumukhi Suresh opened up about representing India at the Netflix Is A Joke Festival in Los Angeles and the emotional moment she learned about the opportunity. She also spoke about Hormonal, Pushpavalli, The Royals 2 and staying relevant.

Rashmika Mandanna, Kriti Sanon and question of 'pehla pyaar' at Cocktail 2 launch Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon, the new Cocktail girls in Bollywood, discussed the idea of first love at the trailer launch of their film. And there's more to the story.

Toxic star Yash video-calls fan battling cancer, offers hope and support Kannada star Yash took a moment from his busy schedule to video-call a fan battling cancer, offering hope and support. His warm interaction and assurance have deeply touched fans across social media.

Rs 84 in account, no auto fare: How Dhurandhar's Gaurav Gera survived struggle days Gaurav Gera has recalled the years before Dhurandhar, when he survived on Rs 84 in his bank account in Mumbai. He said family support, self-respect and caution against arrogance shaped his journey.

Deepika holds back tears, Ranveer enjoys traditions in new pics from family wedding Deepika Padukone got emotional, while Ranveer Singh participated in rituals at his cousin's wedding. The viral photos from the ceremony have renewed attention to the couple's personal life amid news about their professional projects.

Triptii Dimri, Madhuri Dixit on women being judged in cinema: It's not fair Triptii Dimri and Madhuri Dixit spoke about the scrutiny they have faced as Maa Behen nears release. Their accounts from different generations underline how women in cinema are still judged more harshly.

Cocktail 2 trailer: Friendship, flirt, fabulous looks in Shahid-Kriti-Rashmika film The trailer of Cocktail 2 introduces a romantic drama starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, set against a picturesque Italy backdrop. The film explores a complicated love triangle filled with friendship, jealousy and emotional conflict.

Caretaker held for stealing Rs 25 lakh from Raveena Tandon's brother's house Actor Raveena Tandon's family has been caught in a theft case after Juhu police arrested a woman for allegedly stealing jewellery and valuables from the residence of her brother, Rajiv Tandon.

Trisha shares witty post as link-up rumours with CM Vijay continue to fuel trolls Actor Trisha Krishnan shared an Instagram Story featuring her dog with a pointed caption. The post drew notice as online speculation around her equation with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay continued.

general news.headline: Kuwait intercepts missiles and drones, warns public as Iranian media claims retaliationKuwait's military warned residents not to approach debris or unidentified objects after air defences intercepted hostile missiles and drones. The advisory came as Iran's state media claimed US military bases in Kuwait were targeted following recent American actions in the Gulf region.

US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Alive and still in charge? Marco Rubio reveals new details on Mojtaba KhameneiMarco Rubio told senators Mojtaba Khamenei appears increasingly involved in governing Iran. The comments came as Washington and Tehran traded conflicting signals over nuclear talks and sanctions.

Girls locked in cages, raped by 700 men: UK MP shares Pak grooming gang testimoniesThe British MP read out harrowing accounts of systematic sexual abuse, violence, intimidation, racial targeting and alleged police misconduct, alongside claims that public authorities, healthcare workers and children's home staff repeatedly failed to protect vulnerable children.

Pakistanis build mosque illegally in Japan. Embarrassments followThe mosque in Japan's Kawagoe city was inaugurated by Pakistan's Ambassador to Japan, Abdul Hameed, in April. Weeks later, the Pakistani mission was forced to issue a clarification after it emerged that the structure had been built without the necessary approvals from local authorities. Locals are now demanding the structure be razed.

US to screen Iran Soccer World Cup delegation for IRGC-linked membersUS Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Washington will closely scrutinise Iran's Soccer World Cup delegation for possible ties to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). While the Iranian team and support staff are welcome, officials with suspected IRGC links will not be allowed entry.

Firing outside Khan Sir's coaching centre in Patna, rivalry suspectedA firing incident was reported outside educator Khan Sir's coaching centre in Patna's Musallahpur Haat area on Tuesday. Police have launched an investigation, with initial reports suggesting the firing may be linked to rivalry and competition among coaching institutes in the area.

Annamalai tells BJP leaders he will resign, party asks him to wait: SourcesDespite the growing speculation, BJP leaders remain hopeful that Annamalai can be persuaded to stay. Party insiders acknowledge that his departure would be a major setback for the BJP's long-term ambitions in Tamil Nadu, where he is widely credited with energising the cadre base and giving the party unprecedented visibility.

I cracked 5 hackathons and earned Rs 8 lakh before completing my second year of BTechAgnik Misra is a second-year BTech student in Computer Science and Engineering. In just 24 months, he has won five hackathons, secured multiple internships, and earned nearly Rs 8 lakh. He is also working on his own startup.

Resign, Balen: Nepal students, Opposition protest against PM's India remarkPrime Minister Balen Shah's remark that Nepal too might have crossed into Indian territory has triggered a political firestorm, seemingly uniting a fractured opposition, now asking for his resignation. Balen, who rode a wave of protests to become the PM, is himself facing huge protests.

Termite-damaged cash worth Rs 1 crore found in Kolkata college roomBundles of termite-damaged currency notes, reportedly including Rs 100 and Rs 500 notes, were recovered from two suitcases found inside a college union room at Surendranath College during a cleanliness drive in Kolkata.

Lokhande Sitaram named CBSE chief, Varun Bhardwaj Secretary after OSM rowWhat began as complaints over CBSE's on-screen marking system has now led to an inquiry, a leadership shake-up and a political battle over who should be held accountable.

Student databases for Rs 1,500? The dark side of India's exam boomAs CBSE's OSM controversy and NTA portal allegations raise questions about data security, Career360 founder Maheshwer Peri has pointed to a larger issue: student databases allegedly sold for as little as Rs 1,500. The claim brings to focus India's growing student-data economy and the businesses built around it.

Talks still on: Trump counters Iran's claims that peace negotiations have frozenDonald Trump dismissed reports claiming that the US and Iran had stopped communicating in recent days, calling them "false and erroneous".

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.
Explosions heard near Iran's Qeshm Island, according to reportsExplosions were heard near Iran's Qeshm Island, according to reports

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

Final push that made Annamalai plan BJP exit, form new partyDynamic IPS officer-turned-politician K Annamalai is planning to quit the BJP and launch a party. While his exit might appear to be sudden, it comes amid a series of developments ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. Here's what might have been the final push.

Trump to attend rescheduled White House correspondents' dinner on July 24Donald Trump said he will attend the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Association dinner on July 24 after the April event was postponed following a shooting. Organisers said the dinner will go ahead with tighter security and stand against violence and intimidation of the free press.

Siddaramaiah appointed to Congress Working Committee ahead of DK Shivakumar oathAs Karnataka prepares for a leadership transition, the Congress high command has made a significant organisational move that could shape the party's power balance and internal coordination in the state.

45-litre tank, 52 litres of petrol: Kanpur car owner alleges fuel pump scamA car owner in Uttar Pradesh's Kanpur has claimed that a local petrol pump billed him for 52 litres of petrol while topping up a car with a 45-litre tank. District officials have begun a probe into the man's allegations.

All eyes on Omar Abdullah's closed-door MLAs' meet amid statehood, revolt rumoursOmar Abdullah has called NC MLAs and supporting independents for a mandatory meeting in Srinagar. The undeclared agenda and Congress's absence have intensified speculation over statehood and the government's stability.

Will 50 MLAs split TMC, claim symbol? Secret hotel meeting sparks buzzThe threat of a split within the Trinamool Congress is for real and Mamata Banerjee could lose the party's control and symbol. Suspended TMC spokesperson Riju Dutta said that around 50 MLAs, enough to tide over the anti-defection law, had held a secret meeting, and were backing Ritabrata Banerjee as their leader.

Siddaramaiah to retain CM bungalow, DK Shivakumar will move to another govt houseSiddaramaiah will continue in the Cauvery bungalow even as DK Shivakumar takes over as Chief Minister. The arrangement leaves the official residence unchanged while the new Chief Minister shifts to another government house.

sports.headline: Zverev overcomes teen scare to enter French Open semi-finals French Open: Second seed Alexander Zverev stormed into the semi-finals of Roland Garros after beating talented 19-year-old Rafael Jodar. The Spaniard gave Zverev a run for his money in the opening set but faded out later.

Suryakumar Yadav's poor run continues, fails in Mumbai T20 League after IPL India captain Suryakumar Yadav's struggles continued outside the Indian Premier League after the batter failed to assert his dominance in the city-based Mumbai T20 league. Chasing 210 runs vs Ajinkya Rahane's North Mumbai Panthers, Suryakumar got out for just 19 runs.

Sachin Tendulkar analyses IPL 2026 in detail, advocates new powerplay rule Legendary Sachin Tendulkar has analysed the Indian Premier League's 2026 season in detail. Taking to Reddit, Sachin has opened up on the experience of watching Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the distinct identities the teams have taken, and has even advocated a new powerplay rule.

Kostyuk dedicates maiden Grand Slam semi-final to difficult nights in Ukraine Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk gave an emotional speech after reaching her first Grand Slam semi-final. Kostyuk beat her compatriot Elina Svitolina in a 3-set battle to reach the semis of the French Open.

Lakshya Sen knocked out in first round, Kidambi Srikanth's horror run continues PV Sindhu opened with a straight-games win at the Indonesia Open as India faltered elsewhere. Her return to the top 10 stood out on a day of early exits across singles and doubles.

Gautam Gambhir's coaching unit gets new hand for young spinners The BCCI has appointed Sairaj Bahutule as India's spin-bowling coach under Gautam Gambhir. He will mentor a young group of spinners as India try to rebuild their home Test edge.

Snoop Dogg's gospel: Why 19-year-old Andreeva always thanks herself at Slams Mirra Andreeva swept past Sorana Cirstea to reach another French Open semi-final and then explained her habit of thanking herself after big wins. The victory added a teenage Roland Garros record to her run.

PV Sindhu returns to top 10 in badminton rankings after nearly 3 years PV Sindhu returned to the world top 10 and beat Busanan Ongbamrungphan in straight games at the Indonesia Open. The result reinforced her recent revival before a fresh test against world No. 1 An Se-young.

India favourites for T20 World Cup: Ex-Indian captain makes bold claim Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Anjum Chopra has backed India to begin the T20 World Cup in England as one of the favourites. Her view rests on the squad's growing familiarity with conditions and the need for a more flexible batting order.

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

Quarterfinals Highlights: Zverev moves into semis with clinical show vs JodarFrench Open 2026 Highlights: 7th seed Elena Svitolina has crashed out of the French Open after losing to 23-year-old Marta Kostyuk in the quarter-final of the tournament. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev beats Rafael Jodar in straight sets to move into his 5th Roland Garros semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland face tricky continental groupFIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland head a volatile Group B featuring Bosnia and Herzegovina, Canada and Qatar. The section will turn on veteran leaders, injury concerns and very little margin for error.

Man City's Rodri to decide future after World Cup as Real Madrid interest growsFIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri has parked questions over his Manchester City future until after Spain's World Cup campaign. His message to Spain is clear: work humbly, chase every ball, and ignore the noise.

After selection uproar, Auqib Nabi joins India's Test squad but with a twistIND vs AFG One-off Test: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Test squad sparked widespread debate, but the BCCI has now handed the Jammu and Kashmir pacer an unexpected opportunity, offering a twist to the selection story.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find himIPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Roland Garros Day 10 Order of Play: Zverev and next-gen stars take centre stageRoland Garros moves into a loaded day with Alexander Zverev, Elena Svitolina and Joao Fonseca all on the schedule. The draw also carries a Ukrainian derby, a late-night clash of disruptors and Indian interest in boys doubles.

Virat Kohli, RCB fired up as Mo Bobat calls for unprecedented IPL featIPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru ignited dreams of an unprecedented IPL three-peat after team director Mo Bobat delivered a stirring dressing-room rallying cry following their 2026 title victory over Gujarat Titans.

PV Sindhu wins Indonesia Open opener, potential An Se-young clash aheadPV Sindhu opened her Indonesia Open campaign with a straight-games win over Busanan Ongbamrungphan. The result sends her into the next round, where world No. 1 An Se-young is the likely challenge.

Franchise cricket's boom forces ICC to take action on jam-packed calendarThe ICC has set up a committee to examine how the surge of franchise leagues can coexist with the international calendar. The move reflects growing concern that bilateral cricket is losing ground as players and boards follow the T20 money.

Arnaldi outlasts Tiafoe in 5-hour epic to seal historic Italian trio in ParisRoland Garros: Matteo Arnaldi outlasted Frances Tiafoe in a five-set French Open epic to reach the quarter-finals. The comeback completed a rare Italian trio in the last eight and set up a meeting with Matteo Berrettini.

Sabalenka and Osaka unite after Roland Garros clash, want women in prime-time slotsRoland Garros 2026: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka formed a united front at French Open, demanding more prime-time visibility for women's tennis after their historic fourth-round night clash ended a three-year shutout.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget herRajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Sabalenka aces Michael Jackson's moonwalk after beating Osaka: VideoAryna Sabalenka beat Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-3 to reach the French Open quarter-finals before breaking into a moonwalk on Court Philippe-Chatrier. The celebratory flourish capped another commanding display from the World No. 1.

Contract crisis hits ISL as futures of nearly 150 players hang in the balanceNearly 150 ISL players became free agents after their contracts expired amid the AIFF-club stand-off over next season. The ongoing uncertainty has weakened players' bargaining power and hampered clubs' preparations for the upcoming campaign.

Berrettini avenges Jannik Sinner, knocks Cerundolo out in straight setsItalian Matteo Berrettini avenged his compatriot Jannik Sinner in the French Open after beating young Juan Manuel Cerundolo on Monday, June 1. Cerundolo's impressive run in the tournament was stopped in the fourth round.

World Cup: South Africa squad departs without assistant coach after US visa delaySouth Africa's World Cup squad departed for Mexico on Monday, 24 hours later than scheduled, after visa complications disrupted the team's travel plans and left assistant coach Helman Mkhalele stranded in Johannesburg.

Thomas Tuchel backs England to overcome heat and humidity at FIFA World CupEngland manager Thomas Tuchel has backed his side to cope with the heat and humidity expected at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, insisting the challenging conditions in North America will be an obstacle to overcome rather than an excuse for underperformance.

Mischievous RCB stars pour ice-cold water on coach after IPL winRoyal Challengers Bengaluru celebrated their IPL win in style at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. RCB players Tim David and Salt emptied the entire ice bucket on head coach Andy Flower after the final.

Tim David suspended for RCB's IPL 2027 opener after ice-bag incident with umpireRoyal Challengers Bengaluru batter Tim David has been suspended for the franchise's opening match of IPL 2027 after breaching the league's Code of Conduct during the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
